data.title: NovoCure surges on guidance raise, Q2 beats
data.description:
• NovoCure (NVCR) is up ~25% in Thursday morning trading after posting Q2 results that beat on both lines and raising full-year revenue guidance.
  • The oncology company now sees 2026 revenue of $710M-$725M, up from $690M-$710M prior. Consensus is $708.73M.
  • In the quarter, the company's largest Tumor Treating Fields therapy, Optune Gio, saw an 11% increase in active patients to 4,636. Optune Lua patients rose 51% to 207. Also, there were 285 active patients on Optune Pax in the US at the end of the quarter (June 30).
  • NovoCure ended the quarter with cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments of $440.6M compared to $447.7M on Dec. 31, 2025.

data.title: Parnassus Value Equity Fund adds KO; exits BIO
data.description:
• Parnassus Value Equity Fund (PARWX) returned 17.86% in Q2 2026, outperforming the Russell 1000 Value Index, which returned 13.87%, with technology investments leading gains during the quarter.
  • The fund initiated a new position in Coca-Cola (NYSE: KO), citing its strong global brands, pricing power, and long-term growth potential in emerging markets and higher-growth beverage categories.
  • The fund exited its position in Bio-Rad Laboratories (NYSE: BIO), as the stock's risk/reward profile had become less attractive, and redeploying capital into higher-conviction opportunities.
  • Looking ahead, the managers remain cautiously optimistic, expecting quality value stocks to benefit from continued AI infrastructure investment, lower energy prices, and resilient economic conditions while maintaining a focus on quality companies with attractive risk/reward profiles.
  • Source.

data.title: Molina tumbles despite Q2 beat amid high medical expenses
data.description:
Despite reporting better-than-expected financials, Molina Healthcare (MOH) traded lower in the morning hours on Thursday, leading a decline among its managed care peers as analysts focused on its medical costs that exceeded estimates.  

Molina (MOH) fell ~11%, marking its biggest intraday decline since February, while shares of competitors Centene (CNC) and Oscar Health (OSCR), which are yet to report Q2 results, also came under pressure.

On Thursday, Long Beach, California-based Molina (MOH) recorded $1.51 of adjusted EPS for Q2 on $10.9B in revenue, indicating a ~72% YoY and ~5% YoY decline, respectively, but exceeding the consensus by $0.11 and $90M.

The company’s overall medical care ratio reached 92.2% compared to 90.4% in the prior-year period and 92.5% projected by analysts, according to Bloomberg data.

However, its Marketplace business saw a spike in medical costs with MCR reaching 88.9% from 85.4% in Q2 2025 and surpassing 83.9% in the consensus, while membership in the unit also dropped to 230K with nearly a 60% decline from the prior year's period.

Baird analyst Michael Ha, with a Neutral rating on MOH, said headwinds in the Marketplace business remain a matter of concern. “We believe the primary focus of the MOH investment debate continues to be 2027 earnings power,” Bloomberg reported, quoting Ha.

Jefferies analyst David Windley, with a Hold rating, noted multiple issues in the company’s Marketplace business, which he said brings its viability into question. Molina’s (MOH) Marketplace enrollees “also declined slightly more than our estimates, meaning the (40%) organic attrition assumption seems likely, despite the overall market attriting more slowly," the analyst added.

data.title: AppFolio rises as Guggenheim starts with Buy rating
data.description:
• Shares of property management software firm AppFolio (APPF) rose 1.8% in early trading on Thursday as Guggenheim started coverage with a Buy rating and a $232 price target.
  • “We believe the current stock price grossly undervalues the intrinsic value of future cash flows, as it implies overly conservative assumptions in the terminal (or perpetual) period of our DCF,” analyst Tamjid Chowdhury wrote in a note to clients. “We estimate the current valuation implies 0% revenue growth (vs. our assumption of +5%) and/or a significantly impaired FCF margin profile (vs. our assumption of 35%) in the perpetual period. Investor sentiment has soured on the broader application software space, including vertical application software like AppFolio. However, not all verticals are created equal, nor will every software vendor face the same degree of threat from AI. We believe AppFolio is well positioned to, at minimum, preserve its relevance despite the emergence of AI—and potentially capitalize on the opportunity, though the latter remains to be seen.” 
  • Chowdhury continued: “From a numerical standpoint, we believe the company can achieve consensus estimates over the coming years, though upside potential appears more limited in 2026 relative to 2027. The macro backdrop for AppFolio's end market also remains favorable as prolonged elevated interest rates have made renting real estate more economical than owning. That said, recent increases in vacancy rates may partially offset this tailwind. Given these factors, we believe the stock should trade closer to our DCF-derived price target of $232, implying an EV/NTM Recurring Revenue multiple of 7.4x versus the current multiple of approximately 5.1x.”

data.title: Brent crude crosses $100/bbl as Houthi attack on Saudi tankers amplifies oil supply risks
data.description:
Brent crude oil reached $100/bbl for the first time in two months after Iran-backed Houthi militants claimed attacks on two Saudi tankers in the Red Sea, threatening deeper oil supply disruptions and sparking fears that the U.S.-Iran conflict could escalate further.

Front-month Brent crude (CL1:COM) for September delivery soared 6.6% to $100.36/bbl, and front-month Nymex September crude (CO1:COM) jumped 5.4% to $91.53/bbl, adding on to four consecutive daily gains for both benchmarks.

The Houthis said they attacked two Saudi tankers attempting to transit the Bab al-Mandeb strait that connects the Red Sea to the Indian Ocean—although the U.K. Maritime Trade Operations confirmed one of the attacks—after the U.S. completed the 12th straight day of strikes against targets in Iran on Wednesday.

The Houthi attacks open a new front in the conflict, as targeting the Red Sea imperils the critical detour route the Saudis have used to keep oil barrels flowing during the disruption that largely closed off traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.

When the Bab al-Mandeb Strait is shut, vessels can still reach customers in Asia, but only after taking a massive detour through the Suez Canal and around Africa, adding 10-15 days to the journey, according to ship tracker Kpler.

When fully loaded, the biggest tankers sit too deep in the water to pass through the Suez Canal, so shippers would need to operate supertankers on partial loads.

The longer and less efficient journeys will tie up more tanker capacity and push up shipping costs for Asian refiners.

The latest developments in the Middle East come as exports from major producer Kazakhstan are endangered by the Russia-Ukraine war, and the world's oil inventories are depleted.

Meanwhile, The Wall Street Journal reported the U.S. is sending more forces, medics, and weaponry to the Middle East to provide military options as President Trump considers expanding the war.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (XLE)

data.title: Dept. of Justice to speed up antitrust reviews of deals - WSJ
data.description:
The Department of Justice's antitrust division is expected to announce a new model for streamlining deal reviews.

The antitrust division will announce on Thursday a new way for merger reviews that quickly focuses on the most obvious way a deal could hurt competition, according to a  WSJ report on Thursday, which cited department officials.

Under the revised guidelines, the antitrust division will commit to seeking records about a transaction from fewer executives or employees, according to the report. The regulator will decide whether to close or expand a deal probe within about five weeks of receiving corporate records. The department has already used the streamlined approach in two deals this year.

"Streamlining the second request process, where appropriate, is a commonsense way to reduce administrative burden without undermining DOJ's investigations," Associate Attorney General Stanley Woodward said.

The U.S. Justice Department's antitrust division approved Paramount Skydance's (PSKY) planned $110 billion purchase of Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) last month.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: TSLA, GOOG, AAL, and more
data.description:
Stock futures inched lower on Thursday as investors worked through a fresh wave of technology earnings while keeping a close eye on escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Hyliion (HYLN) +14% - Shares surged after the company secured a $41.7M U.S. Navy contract from the Office of Naval Research to design, develop, build, test, and deliver 2-megawatt and 3-megawatt KARNO power generation systems for locations designated by the agency. This marks a significant validation of Hyliion's KARNO technology by the government.
  • United Rentals (URI) +13% - Shares jumped after the equipment rental company beat Q2 revenue and earnings estimates and raised its full-year guidance on continued strength in large construction and industrial projects. Revenue reached a record $4.41B, while adjusted EPS of $12.76 topped expectations. The company also lifted its FY2026 revenue, EBITDA, operating cash flow, and capital spending outlooks, citing strong customer demand, healthy project backlogs, and year-to-date momentum.
  • Lockheed Martin (LMT) +11% - Shares rallied after the defense contractor beat Q2 revenue and earnings estimates and raised its full-year guidance on strong demand for missile systems and military aircraft. Revenue climbed 11% to $20.1B, driven by higher production of F-35 fighters, THAAD, Patriot PAC-3, and Precision Strike Missiles, while the company secured $65B in new orders, lifting its backlog to a record $230B. Lockheed also increased its 2026 sales, EPS, operating profit, and free cash flow outlook, reflecting sustained strength in global defense spending.
  • RTX (RTX) +8% - Shares surged after the aerospace and defense company beat Q2 revenue and earnings estimates and raised its full-year guidance on strong demand across its commercial aerospace and defense businesses. Revenue climbed 14% to $24.7B, while adjusted EPS of $1.89 topped expectations. RTX also lifted its 2026 sales, organic growth, EPS, and free cash flow outlooks, supported by robust performance at Pratt & Whitney, Collins Aerospace, and Raytheon, as its backlog reached a record $289B.
  • ServiceNow (NOW) +5% - Shares rose after the enterprise software company beat Q2 revenue and earnings estimates and raised its full-year subscription revenue outlook. Revenue climbed 24% Y/Y to $3.98B, while adjusted EPS of $0.90 topped expectations. ServiceNow also highlighted ninefold growth in AI agent deployments over the past nine months and increased its 2026 subscription revenue guidance, underscoring continued strong demand for its AI-powered enterprise software platform.

Biggest stock losers

  • Tesla (TSLA) -12% - Shares slid after the EV maker missed Q2 earnings expectations as margin compression and negative free cash flow overshadowed record revenue. EPS of $0.33 missed consensus, while operating margin fell to 1.4%, gross margin slipped to 16.8%, and the company posted negative free cash flow of $1.1B due to elevated capital spending. Tesla reported record quarterly revenue growth of 26% Y/Y and reaffirmed plans to begin Tesla Semi, Megapack 3, and Optimus production in 2026, but weaker profitability weighed on investor sentiment.
  • American Airlines (AAL) -7% - Shares declined despite a Q2 earnings beat after the airline cut its full-year profit outlook and issued weaker-than-expected Q3 guidance, citing sharply higher fuel costs. The company warned that an 83% surge in fuel expenses will weigh on profitability, forecasting a Q3 loss of $0.10-$0.70 per share versus expectations for a $0.31 profit, while its FY2026 EPS outlook also fell short of consensus. The weaker guidance overshadowed continued strength in premium leisure and corporate travel demand.
  • Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) -6% - Shares fell as surging AI spending, weaker-than-expected profitability, and higher capital expenditure guidance overshadowed a strong Q2 earnings beat. Revenue climbed 25% to $119.8B, led by an 82% jump in Google Cloud revenue, but adjusted EPS missed estimates, the operating margin of 34% fell short of expectations, and the company posted negative free cash flow of $5.9B as AI infrastructure spending accelerated. Alphabet also raised its 2026 capex outlook to $195B-$205B from $180B-$190B, fueling concerns over the cost of its AI expansion despite robust cloud growth and a record $514B backlog.

data.title: Sandisk's price target cut at Susquehanna
data.description:
Susquehanna reduced Sandisk's (SNDK) price target to $3,050 from $3,250 while keeping its Positive rating on the shares.

Analysts led by Mehdi Hosseini said they have updated estimates after a correction to their model, which had previously reflected inaccurate revenue and EPS forecasts. 

The analysts also highlighted several key topics for investors ahead of memory maker Sandisk's fiscal fourth quarter results on Aug. 5 and Analyst Day on Aug. 13.

The memory hierarchy for AI inferencing applications remains unsettled. The emerging architecture will help determine what portion of tokens generated during the decoding phase will reside in Dynamic Random-Access Memory, or DRAM, versus local Solid-State Drive, or SSD, the analysts noted.

How the TLC/QLC mix evolves as KV-cache offloading accelerates and drives incremental SSD demand, is also a key point, according to Hosseini and his team.

TLC and QLC are types of flash memory, while Key-Value, or KV, cache offloading is a memory-saving technique for AI large language models, or LLMs. It moves a model's attention data — the memory of previous tokens — from expensive GPU memory into larger but slower CPU RAM or SSD storage.

The ramp of BiCS8 and BiCS10 NAND technologies and their role in driving about 10% annual front-end cost-per-bit reductions; normalized revenue mix between NAND (flash) and SSD products; and timing of HBF sample availability and the profile of customers evaluating the technology, were also among the things highlighted by the analysts.

Other key points included: the percentage of fiscal year 2027 and fiscal year 2028 capacity already committed under contractual agreements and the average duration of these longer-term contracts; revenue growth and margin profiles by end market, including Data Center, Edge, andConsumer segments; and incremental operating profit leverage as gross margin approach peaks near 80%.

Shares of Sandisk rose about 2% on Thursday.

data.title: Procor Technologies initiated with Buy rating at Guggenheim
data.description:
Procor Technologies (PCOR) was initiated with a Buy rating and a $68 price target at Guggenheim on Thursday.

“We believe PCOR sits at an inflection point, facing one of the most significant technological transformations in its history as AI reaches one of the least digitized industries,” analyst Tamjid Chowdhury wrote in a note to clients. “Applying AI to construction will require a fresh lens and a proven technologist, and we believe the company will find that in the recently appointed CEO, Ajei Gopal. In our view, PCOR rests on a strong foundation against AI obsolescence, meeting four of the five criteria we have identified (context rich systems of record + highly regulated industry + multi stakeholder focus + outcome based pricing). From a numerical standpoint, near term estimates present a low bar but not so much in the long term.”

Looking deeper, Chowdhury said that despite the recent softness in construction spending, the underlying economic outlook is actually positive for Procore's future. Backlogs are elevated and the labor market is still tight, both of which benefit Procore, Chowdhury added.

“We believe PCOR can achieve consensus in both the near and long term, but the near-term setup appears more compelling,” Chowdhury added. “We see roughly 200bps of upside potential to 2Q consensus, though the beat magnitude narrows through the year. Longer term, the implied ~14% revenue CAGR from 2025-2028 requires adjusted average New ARR growth of about 11%, well below the 21% 5-year average, but above the 5% average of the last 2 years. Achievable, in our view, but with limited upside. On cRPO, we do not view it as a reliable proxy for business momentum, as it can be skewed by factors such as contract duration and renewal timing, which investors lack visibility into. Net-net, long-term consensus estimates appear achievable, but material upside requires leverage from an improving macro backlog environment.”

data.title: Defense giant RTX jumps 8% on earnings, and these 10 ETFs hold the largest stakes
data.description:
Exchange traded funds with significant exposure to RTX Corporation (RTX) are in focus on Thursday after the defense contractor’s shares climbed 8.2% in early morning trading. The advance follows second-quarter results that surpassed Wall Street expectations, accompanied by a positive earnings report and an increase in full-year guidance.

The strength is reverberating across the ETF marketplace, where 386 funds currently hold positions in RTX totaling 223.4 million shares. Among these, a select group of aerospace and defense-focused funds stands out for the highest portfolio concentrations in the stock. The top-allocated ETFs are outlined below.

  1. iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF (ITA), 15.95% allocation
  2. Direxion Daily Aerospace & Defense Bull 3X Shares (DFEN), 12.65% allocation
  3. Global X Defense Tech ETF (SHLD), 9.5% allocation
  4. Corgi Aerospace & Commercial Aviation ETF (AV), 9.17% allocation
  5. iShares Defense Industrials Active ETF (IDEF), 8.76% allocation
  6. Pacer Solactive Whitney Future of Warfare ETF (FOWF), 8.16% allocation
  7. Themes Transatlantic Defense ETF (NATO), 8.01% allocation
  8. Invesco Aerospace & Defense ETF (PPA), 7.75% allocation
  9. First Trust Indxx Aerospace & Defense ETF (MISL), 7.61% allocation
  10. Parametric Equity Premium Income ETF (PAPI), 7.5% allocation
